qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in signature display endpoint allows arbitrary file read
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a mass assignment vulnerability on user attributes (CWE-915) allowing authenticated users to modify restricted fields and take over admin accounts. This finding describes a path traversal vulnerability in signature display endpoints (CWE-22). Different vulnerability class, different root cause (missing input validation vs insufficient mass assignment protection), different affected code locations (ActionlogController file read paths vs user update endpoints), different attack vector (filename parameter vs user model mass assignment), and different impact (arbitrary file read vs privilege escalation).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying vulnerability finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory (GHSA-5448-v74m-7mv7/CVE-2025-15602) describes a mass assignment vulnerability in Snipe-IT where insufficient protection of sensitive user attributes allows authenticated low-privileged users to modify restricted fields (including email) of other user accounts, enabling account takeover through password reset. The qualifying finding reports a path traversal vulnerability in signature display endpoints allowing arbitrary file read. This finding does not identify the mass assignment vulnerability, the affected user update endpoints, or the missing field protection mechanism described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: Insufficient protection of sensitive user attributes against mass assignment in user update endpoints
Advisory impact: Authenticated low-privileged user can modify restricted fields of other users including Super Admin email, then trigger password reset for account takeover
Advisory CWE: CWE-915 (Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes)
Finding (path_traversal_signature_display): Reports CWE-22 (Path Traversal) in ActionlogController signature display endpoints - different vulnerability class and different code location
No finding identifies user model mass assignment, missing $guarded/$fillable protection on sensitive user fields, or user update API endpoints
